data.reasoning: Starting with process list — memory is most volatile evidence. Anomalous parent-child relationships indicate process injection or masquerading.
data.expected: Normal Windows process tree with services.exe, svchost.exe hierarchy
data.looking_for: Processes spawned from unexpected parents, known malicious names, orphaned processes

data.description: Process cmd.exe (PID 3908) has anomalous parent-child relationship
Process mdd_1.3.exe (PID 2160) has anomalous parent-child relationship

data.reasoning: Checking network connections after process analysis — if suspicious process has external connection, that is strong C2 indicator.
data.expected: Local connections only for a corporate workstation
data.looking_for: External IPs, unusual ports, connections from suspicious PIDs

data.reasoning: Checking registry Run keys — common persistence mechanism. Malware writes here to survive reboot.
data.expected: Legitimate software entries pointing to Program Files
data.looking_for: Entries pointing to temp dirs, encoded paths, unusual executables

data.reasoning: Running malfind after process analysis — code injection leaves executable memory regions with no mapped file. Cross-referencing with suspicious PIDs found earlier.
data.expected: Clean memory regions for all processes
data.looking_for: PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE regions, VAD anomalies, shellcode indicators

data.description: Pat McGoo assigned Nitroba prior art investigation to Charlie via email. 8 assignment-related emails found.
Charlie sent 6 suspicious emails to external parties including andy@swexpert.com and jamie@project2400.com with subjects: 'I Found Something', 'Instructions', 'Picture'.

data.reasoning: Analyzing document artifacts — LNK files reveal recently accessed files. Quantum Cryptography folder and patentauto.py indicate deliberate collection and automation.
data.expected: Work documents related to investigation
data.looking_for: Patent documents, staging folders, automation scripts, exfiltration artifacts

data.description: Charlie created a 'Quantum Cryptography' folder containing 11 patent documents. Files include Korean and GB patent filings. Last modified Dec 10-11 2009.
Charlie created and executed patentauto.py — a Python script to automate patent research. LNK file confirms execution on 2009-11-20.
Charlie actively worked on 'Nitroba work.odt' — the primary investigation document. 5 LNK versions found indicating repeated access.
Charlie accessed M57biz business files. 3 related artifacts found.
Charlie downloaded patent research tools including alternatiff. 4 tool artifacts found.
